item.title: State Street Head Trader Says Won May Rebound in Second Half
item.description: The South Korean won could rebound in the second half of the year as foreign selling of local equities eases and persistent current-account surpluses support the currency, according to a State Street Bank and Trust Co. trader with more than 25 years at the firm.

item.title: Twinco Capital Raises $190 Million for Supply-Chain Financing
item.description: European fintech firm Twinco Capital has raised a total of €165 million ($190 million) in a series B equity round and a securitization fund.

item.title: China’s Solar Industry Launches Space Alliance With Few Details
item.description: Chinese solar manufacturers and research institutes launched an alliance on Tuesday to promote solar energy in space, a potential new avenue for growth in an industry beset by terrestrial overcapacity.

item.title: Russian Aircraft Production Surges as Putin Pivots to Drone War
item.description: Even as the Kremlin’s war machine starts to lose steam after years of relentless expansion, Russia’s aircraft sector is on a tear, fueled by insatiable demand for thousands of drones.

item.title: SpaceX — To Boldly Raise Money Where No IPO Has Gone Before
item.description: The final frontier is efficiency, as some of the world’s biggest companies land on indexes.

item.title: Poland Set to Hold Interest Rates After Inflation Pressure Cools
item.description: Poland’s central bank is poised to keep interest rates unchanged for a third straight month as an unexpected drop in the inflation rate relieves pressure to tighten monetary policy.

item.title: China’s State Buyer Seeks Scrutiny of New Fortescue Iron Ore
item.description: China’s state-backed iron ore buyer has instructed some steel mills to ask questions about Fortescue Ltd.’s new low-grade product as negotiations over a long-term supply contract hit a rough patch.

item.title: Schroders Renewable Unit Targets AI Assets as Power Demand Soars
item.description: Renewable-energy investor Schroders Greencoat is targeting newer data center-linked assets as AI fuels a surge in electricity demand.

item.title: Pimco Says Treasury Yields Driven by Fed Bets, Not AI, for Now
item.description: A boom in artificial intelligence-related borrowing may become a bigger influence on bond markets over time, but the idea that it’s behind a recent rise in long-dated Treasury yields appears overstated, according to Pacific Investment Management Co.

item.title: Japan’s 10-Year Bond Yield Drops After Auction Sees Firm Demand
item.description: Japan’s 10-year government bond yield dropped after an auction of the tenor drew firm demand as investors were attracted to high yields despite the ongoing uncertainty in the Middle East.

item.title: Japan’s Duty-Free Sales Climb in May as Tourism Boom Endures
item.description: Japan’s duty-free sales surged in May as tourists flock to the country to take advantage of a persistently weak yen, helping major department stores offset a sharp decline in visitors from China.

item.title: Nvidia Boss Says Workers Should Be Paid ‘as Much as Possible’
item.description: Nvidia Corp. Chief Executive Officer Jensen Huang said he pays his workers as much as possible, weighing in on a growing global debate about how profits from the AI infrastructure boom should be shared.

item.title: PBOC Cuts Cash Operation to Record Low as Bond Rally Deepens
item.description: China’s central bank reduced the size of its daily open-market operation to a record low, extending efforts to absorb excess cash as a rally in bonds drove benchmark yields to their lowest since August.

item.title: China’s LNG Imports Rebound in May as Buyers Prepare for Summer
item.description: China’s liquefied natural gas imports rebounded in May as the world’s largest buyer stepped up purchases ahead of peak summer demand, reversing a months-long decline following disruptions to Middle East supplies.

item.title: Southeast Asia’s Factories Ramp Up on Robust Domestic Demand
item.description: Southeast Asia’s manufacturing sector snapped a three-month slowdown in May, as a surge in domestic demand helped offset a downturn in exports.

item.title: China Adds Data and AI to Trade Secret Rules to Block Leaks
item.description: China expanded its trade secret rules to include data and algorithms, as Beijing steps up efforts to prevent technology leaks amid intensifying strategic competition with the US.

item.title: Foreign Ownership of Indian Shares Tumbles to 10-Year Low
item.description: International investors now hold less than 15% of Indian equities.

item.title: RBA’s Harper Worries Over ‘Uptick’ in Inflation Expectations
item.description: Australia’s longer-term inflation expectations have edged higher, with financial markets now anticipating consumer prices will remain above the Reserve Bank’s target for an extended period — a shift not seen in years, according to RBA board member Ian Harper.

item.title: Aluminum Advances to Four-Year High on Tighter Global Market
item.description: Aluminum rose for a fourth day to hit its highest since 2022, extending this year’s powerful gain as US President Donald Trump struggles to keep peace talks with Iran on track.

item.title: General Mills to Sell Mainland China Häagen-Dazs Ice-Cream Shops to Local Tea Brand Ningji
item.description: General Mills Inc. has agreed to sell its Häagen-Dazs shops in mainland China to a group including local tea brand Ningji.

item.title: China’s Yuan Rises to Highest Level Against Basket Since 2022
item.description: China’s yuan has strengthened to its highest level in nearly four years versus a basket of trading-partner currencies, underscoring the appeal of Chinese assets as a regional haven during the Iran conflict.

item.title: Chile’s Government Seeks $6.2 Billion in Additional Debt Sales
item.description: Chile’s government is asking Congress to approve additional debt sales this year after widening the budget deficit forecast for 2026 amid a dispute over how the previous administration calculated its forecasts.

item.title: Chinese Bionic Hand Maker Sees Humanoid Robot-Led Sales Boom
item.description: Chinese prosthetics developer BrainCo expects sales of its robotic hands to surge this year as demand grows from the country’s fast-expanding humanoid robotics industry.

item.title: Japan’s Takaichi Urges Passage of Vessels in Call with Iran
item.description: Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi called for the safe passage of all vessels regardless of nation in her third phone call with Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian.

item.title: Australia Raises Minimum Wage Rate by 4.75%, Citing Uncertainty
item.description: Australia’s industrial relations umpire raised national minimum wage rates by 4.75%, just shy of the Reserve Bank’s inflation forecast, citing uncertainty over the economy’s trajectory.

item.title: US Cuts Agricultural Equipment Tariffs on Rising Farm Costs
item.description: The White House said it will reduce tariffs on agricultural equipment, such as combines and harvesters, in order to reduce costs for US farmers and manufacturers.

item.title: Billionaire Kazakh Brokerage CEO Gets SEC Enforcement Warning
item.description: The billionaire CEO of Kazakhstan-based Freedom Holding Corp. has received a so-called Wells Notice from the US Securities and Exchange Commission, a formal warning that the Wall Street regulator may take enforcement action.

item.title: Japan’s Katayama Says Always Ready to Take Steps on Currencies as Needed
item.description: Japanese Finance Minister Satsuki Katayama said authorities are always ready to take steps as needed on currencies — days after the release of data showing that authorities conducted a record spate of currency intervention in the period between late April and late May.

item.title: Congo Reopens Main Airport in Ebola Epicenter as Officials Brace for Long Outbreak
item.description: Congo reopened the main airport in the eastern province hardest hit by Ebola, after health officials reported tentative signs the outbreak may be slowing despite a continuing struggle to trace exposed contacts and investigate suspected cases.

item.title: Optimum Pushes Apollo, Ares Debt Feud to Brink With Asset Shift
item.description: One of the most bitter feuds in distressed-debt investing, between billionaire Patrick Drahi and some of the world’s biggest investors, has suddenly gotten even more contentious.
